summary: Daniel Okafor and James Mitchell from Aegis met with Damien Rowe from Stratos to discuss using Aegis Comply to streamline their compliance preparation ahead of a Q2 (May) audit. Damien explained that their current process is highly manual, requiring engineers to spend weeks pulling evidence across spreadsheets for SOC 2 and ISO 27001 compliance. The Aegis team walked through how Comply continuously maps cloud infrastructure to compliance frameworks and generates audit-ready evidence, noting integration with their existing Aegis Detect product. The call ended positively with agreement to schedule a tailored demo for Damien's engineering and security team.
actionItems: Daniel Okafor: Send Damien a scheduling link for the demo within the week
Daniel Okafor: Prepare a one-pager on SOC 2 and ISO 27001 framework mapping to share with Damien's team ahead of the demo
Damien Rowe: Add Head of Security Priya and a senior DevOps engineer to the demo invite
James Mitchell: Ensure all pre-demo materials are sent to Damien the same day as the call

keyMoments.text: Damien describes a painful, fully manual compliance process costing significant engineering time
keyMoments.speaker: Damien Rowe

keyMoments.text: Damien reacts very positively to continuous evidence collection, calling it 'genuinely huge'
keyMoments.speaker: Damien Rowe

keyMoments.text: James reveals that on-demand reporting is not yet available but is coming in an upcoming release
keyMoments.speaker: James Mitchell

keyMoments.text: Damien confirms existing use of Aegis Detect and asks about integration with Comply, leading to a strong platform cohesion pitch
keyMoments.speaker: Damien Rowe
